results.question: Show best student
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.id, s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department
FROM students s
JOIN (
  SELECT student_id, MAX(marks) as max_marks
  FROM scores
  GROUP BY student_id
) sm ON s.id = sm.student_id
ORDER BY sm.max_marks DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.id, s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department
FROM students s
JOIN (
  SELECT student_id, MAX(marks) as max_marks
  FROM scores
  GROUP BY student_id
) sm ON s.id = sm.student_id
ORDER BY sm.max_marks DESC
LIMIT 1;

results.question: Top performer
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
    s.name, 
    c.title, 
    MAX(s.marks) AS max_marks
FROM 
    scores s
JOIN 
    courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
JOIN 
    students st ON s.student_id = st.id
GROUP BY 
    s.name, 
    c.title
ORDER BY 
    max_marks DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
    st.name, 
    c.title, 
    MAX(s.marks) AS max_marks
FROM 
    scores s
JOIN 
    courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
JOIN 
    students st ON s.student_id = st.id
GROUP BY 
    st.name, 
    c.title
ORDER BY 
    max_marks DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.notes: System recovered baseline failure: no such column: s.name

results.question: High scoring students
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, c.title, SUM(s.marks) as total_marks
FROM scores s
JOIN courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
JOIN students s2 ON s.student_id = s2.id
GROUP BY s.name, s.email, c.title
ORDER BY total_marks DESC
LIMIT 10;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, c.title, SUM(s.marks) as total_marks
FROM scores s
JOIN courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
JOIN students s2 ON s.student_id = s2.id
GROUP BY s.student_id, s2.name, s2.email, c.title
ORDER BY total_marks DESC
LIMIT 10;
results.notes: Both failed (baseline: no such column: s.name; system: no such column: s.name)

results.question: List student emails with course names and marks above average
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
    s.email, 
    c.title, 
    s.marks
FROM 
    scores s
JOIN 
    courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
WHERE 
    s.marks > (SELECT AVG(marks) FROM scores);
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
    s.student_name, 
    c.title, 
    s.marks
FROM 
    scores s
JOIN 
    courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
WHERE 
    s.marks > (SELECT AVG(marks) FROM scores);
results.notes: Both failed (baseline: no such column: s.email; system: no such column: s.student_name)

results.question: Students who performed better than average in each course
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
  c.code, 
  c.title, 
  AVG(s.marks) AS avg_marks, 
  AVG(s.marks) + 1 AS threshold
FROM 
  scores s
  JOIN courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
GROUP BY 
  c.code, 
  c.title
HAVING 
  AVG(s.marks) > (SELECT AVG(marks) FROM scores);
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
  c.code, 
  c.title, 
  AVG(s.marks) AS avg_marks, 
  AVG(s.marks) + 1 AS threshold
FROM 
  scores s
  JOIN courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
GROUP BY 
  c.code, 
  c.title
HAVING 
  AVG(s.marks) > (SELECT AVG(marks) FROM scores);

results.question: Find students who scored above average in at least 2 courses
results.baseline_sql:
WITH 
  -- Calculate average marks for each student
  student_avg_marks AS (
    SELECT 
      s.id, 
      AVG(s.marks) AS avg_marks
    FROM 
      scores s
    GROUP BY 
      s.student_id
  ),
  
  -- Find students who scored above average in at least 2 courses
  top_students AS (
    SELECT 
      s.id, 
      COUNT(CASE WHEN s.marks > sa.avg_marks THEN 1 END) AS above_avg_count
    FROM 
      scores s
    JOIN 
      student_avg_marks sa ON s.student_id = sa.id
    GROUP BY 
      s.student_id
    HAVING 
      COUNT(CASE WHEN s.marks > sa.avg_marks THEN 1 END) >= 2
  )

SELECT 
  s.id, 
  s.name, 
  s.email
FROM 
  students s
JOIN 
  top_students ts ON s.id = ts.id;
results.system_sql:
WITH 
  -- Calculate average marks for each student
  student_avg_marks AS (
    SELECT 
      s.id, 
      AVG(s.marks) AS avg_marks
    FROM 
      scores s
    GROUP BY 
      s.student_id
  ),
  
  -- Find students who scored above average in at least 2 courses
  top_students AS (
    SELECT 
      s.id, 
      COUNT(CASE WHEN s.marks > sa.avg_marks THEN 1 END) AS above_avg_count
    FROM 
      scores s
    JOIN 
      student_avg_marks sa ON s.student_id = sa.id
    GROUP BY 
      s.student_id
    HAVING 
      COUNT(CASE WHEN s.marks > sa.avg_marks THEN 1 END) >= 2
  )

SELECT 
  s.id, 
  s.name, 
  s.email
FROM 
  students s
JOIN 
  top_students ts ON s.id = ts.id;

results.question: Top department based on average marks
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
    s.department, 
    AVG(s.marks) AS avg_marks
FROM 
    scores s
JOIN 
    students st ON s.student_id = st.id
GROUP BY 
    s.department
ORDER BY 
    avg_marks DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
    st.department, 
    AVG(s.marks) AS avg_marks
FROM 
    scores s
JOIN 
    students st ON s.student_id = st.id
GROUP BY 
    st.department
ORDER BY 
    avg_marks DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.notes: System recovered baseline failure: no such column: s.department

results.question: student_name list
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
    s.name AS student_name
FROM 
    students s
JOIN 
    scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
GROUP BY 
    s.name
ORDER BY 
    s.name;
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
    s.name AS student_name
FROM 
    students s
JOIN 
    scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id

    s.name
ORDER BY 
    s.name;

results.question: course score data
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
    c.code, 
    c.title, 
    s.marks, 
    s.exam_date
FROM 
    scores s
INNER JOIN 
    courses c ON s.course_id = c.id;
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
    c.code, 
    c.title, 
    s.marks, 
    s.exam_date
FROM 
    scores s
INNER JOIN 
    courses c ON s.course_id = c.id;

results.question: List students whose average marks are above the overall average
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.*
FROM students s
JOIN (
  SELECT student_id, AVG(marks) AS avg_marks
  FROM scores
  GROUP BY student_id
) sm ON s.id = sm.student_id
WHERE sm.avg_marks > (
  SELECT AVG(marks) AS overall_avg
  FROM scores
);
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.*
FROM students s
JOIN (
  SELECT student_id, AVG(marks) AS avg_marks
  FROM scores
  GROUP BY student_id
) sm ON s.id = sm.student_id
WHERE sm.avg_marks > (
  SELECT AVG(marks) AS overall_avg
  FROM scores
);

results.question: Show course-wise toppers with student names
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
    c.code, 
    c.title, 
    s.name, 
    s.id AS student_id, 
    s.email, 
    s.age, 
    s.department, 
    MAX(s.marks) AS max_marks
FROM 
    scores s
JOIN 
    courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
JOIN 
    students st ON s.student_id = st.id
GROUP BY 
    c.code, 
    c.title, 
    s.name, 
    s.id, 
    s.email, 
    s.age, 
    s.department
ORDER BY 
    c.code, 
    max_marks DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
    c.code, 
    c.title, 
    st.name AS student_name, 
    st.id AS student_id, 
    st.email, 
    st.age, 
    st.department, 
    MAX(s.marks) AS max_marks
FROM 
    scores s
JOIN 
    courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
JOIN 
    students st ON s.student_id = st.id
GROUP BY 
    c.code, 
    c.title, 
    st.name, 
    st.id, 
    st.email, 
    st.age, 
    st.department
ORDER BY 
    c.code, 
    max_marks DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.notes: System recovered baseline failure: no such column: s.name

results.question: Count students scoring above 90 in each course
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
    c.code, 
    c.title, 
    COUNT(s.id) as total_students
FROM 
    scores s
JOIN 
    courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
WHERE 
    s.marks > 90
GROUP BY 
    c.code, 
    c.title;
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
    c.code, 
    c.title, 
    COUNT(s.id) as total_students
FROM 
    scores s
JOIN 
    courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
WHERE 
    s.marks > 90
GROUP BY 
    c.code, 
    c.title;

results.question: Show top 3 students per course
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
  c.code, 
  c.title, 
  s.name, 
  s.id AS student_id, 
  s.email, 
  s.age, 
  s.department
FROM 
  scores sc
JOIN 
  students s ON sc.student_id = s.id
JOIN 
  courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
GROUP BY 
  c.code, 
  c.title, 
  s.name, 
  s.id, 
  s.email, 
  s.age, 
  s.department
ORDER BY 
  c.code, 
  s.name DESC
LIMIT 3;
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
  c.code, 
  c.title, 
  st.name, 
  st.id AS student_id, 
  st.email, 
  st.age, 
  st.department
FROM 
  scores s
JOIN 
  courses c ON s.course_id = c.id
JOIN 
  students st ON s.student_id = st.id
GROUP BY 
  c.code, 
  c.title, 
  st.name, 
  st.id, 
  st.email, 
  st.age, 
  st.department
ORDER BY 
  c.code, 
  s.marks DESC
LIMIT 3;

results.question: Find students with highest marks in each department
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
    s.name, 
    s.department, 
    MAX(s.marks) AS highest_marks
FROM 
    students s
JOIN 
    scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
GROUP BY 
    s.department, 
    s.name
ORDER BY 
    s.department, 
    highest_marks DESC;
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
    s.name, 
    s.department, 
    MAX(sc.marks) AS highest_marks
FROM 
    students s
JOIN 
    scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
GROUP BY 
    s.department, 
    s.name
ORDER BY 
    s.department, 
    highest_marks DESC;
results.notes: System recovered baseline failure: no such column: s.marks

results.question: List students whose marks improved over time
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s1.name, s1.email, s1.age, s1.department, s1.id AS student_id, 
       c.title AS course_title, s1.marks AS prev_marks, s2.marks AS curr_marks
FROM (
  SELECT s.id, s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department, s.marks, 
         LAG(s.marks) OVER (PARTITION BY s.id ORDER BY s.exam_date) AS prev_marks
  FROM scores s
) s1
JOIN scores s2 ON s1.id = s2.student_id AND s1.exam_date < s2.exam_date
JOIN courses c ON s2.course_id = c.id
WHERE s1.marks < s2.marks;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s1.name, s1.email, s1.age, s1.department, s1.id AS student_id, 
       c.title AS course_title, s1.prev_marks AS prev_marks, s2.marks AS curr_marks
FROM (
  SELECT s.id, s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department, s.marks, 
         LAG(s.marks) OVER (PARTITION BY s.id ORDER BY s.exam_date) AS prev_marks
  FROM scores s
) s1
JOIN scores s2 ON s1.id = s2.id AND s1.exam_date < s2.exam_date
JOIN courses c ON s2.course_id = c.id
WHERE s1.prev_marks IS NOT NULL AND s1.prev_marks < s2.marks;
results.notes: Both failed (baseline: no such column: s.name; system: no such column: s.name)

results.question: Show departments with more than 5 students scoring above 80
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT 
    s.department
FROM 
    students s
JOIN 
    scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
GROUP BY 
    s.department
HAVING 
    COUNT(s.id) > 5 AND AVG(sc.marks) > 80;
results.system_sql:
SELECT 
    s.department
FROM 
    students s
JOIN 
    scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
GROUP BY 
    s.department
HAVING 
    COUNT(s.id) > 5 AND AVG(sc.marks) > 80;

results.question: Courses taken by all Computer Science students
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT c.code, c.title, c.credits
FROM courses c
JOIN scores s ON c.id = s.course_id
JOIN students st ON s.student_id = st.id
WHERE st.department = 'Computer Science';
results.system_sql:
SELECT c.code, c.title, c.credits
FROM courses c
JOIN scores s ON c.id = s.course_id
JOIN students st ON s.student_id = st.id
WHERE st.department = 'Computer Science';

results.question: Students who scored in every course
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.id, s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department
FROM students s
JOIN (
  SELECT student_id
  FROM scores
  GROUP BY student_id
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT course_id) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM courses)
) sc ON s.id = sc.student_id;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.id, s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department
FROM students s
JOIN (
  SELECT student_id
  FROM scores
  GROUP BY student_id
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT course_id) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM courses)
) sc ON s.id = sc.student_id;
